title: record_validation_resolution data payload
description: v0.31.0 active-learning feedback loop. Persists a human-confirmed verdict (positive/negative/uncertain) on a tablet pair to ~/.cache/cuneiform-mcp/validation-resolutions.json. Each positive grows the labeled set toward the v1.0 ≥100-positives threshold (currently bootstrapped at n=12 from methods-paper hardcoded list). Closes one of the v1.0-readiness-checklist gates organically as Dane works the prioritize_validation_queue ranker. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

action.description: 'created' for a new pair; 'updated' if a prior verdict for the same canonical pair_id was overwritten.

previous.description: The prior resolution that was overwritten when action='updated', or null when action='created'.

cache_path.description: Absolute path of the JSON store file.

pair_id.description: Canonical pair identifier — '{tablet_a}↔{tablet_b}' with tablet_a < tablet_b lexicographically. Ensures the same pair is never double-stored as (A,B) and (B,A).

verdict.description: Human-confirmed relationship classification. 'positive' = sibling/host/recension-member pair worth feeding to the Bayesian fusion training set. 'negative' = false-positive surfaced by the queue. 'uncertain' = needs more eyeballs (e.g. fragmentary witness).

rationale.description: Free-form scholar note — why this verdict was assigned. Surfaces in list_validation_resolutions output and propagates into the eventual labeled-pair audit trail.

recorded_by.description: Free-form identifier of the recorder (default 'manual'). Use an email or initials for multi-scholar projects.

source.description: Which path produced the verdict. 'validation_queue' = surfaced by prioritize_validation_queue. 'methods_paper' = curated from §3.x findings. 'audit_resolution' = closing out an audit-script discovery. 'user_manual' = ad-hoc.

methods_paper_section.description: Optional methods-paper section reference (e.g. '§3.7.3') if the verdict ties to a published claim.

tool_version.description: VERSION constant at time of record — lets future re-trains flag verdicts made under stale tool calibrations.

bootstrap_positives_from_methods_paper.description: Positives hardcoded in scripts/train-joint-pair-model.mjs (currently 12). Persisted resolutions add to this count toward v1.0.
